response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with genuine developer-tooling interest — including direct IDE development on NI LabWindows/CVI and authoring test runners and assertion libraries — but the core stack does not match. Their career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, with only incidental Java exposure and no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or the enterprise JVM ecosystem the role requires. Retraining into deep JVM/Spring expertise for a mid-senior IC role focused on enterprise Java tooling would be a significant stretch.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Strong developer-tooling and testing background (test runner, assertion library, TDD/craftsmanship practices)
Broad, long-tenured engineering experience across web, embedded, and high-throughput systems
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience — both core required qualifications
Java exposure is minimal and dated (secondary language on 2016-2018 projects)
Recent work (TypeScript/Node, AI tooling) is far from enterprise JVM frameworks and application servers
